extra_model_fields.contact_for_questions: If unclear which records should be marked 'needs_review', flag the record and include a short rationale; the verifier will confirm.
extra_model_fields.fallback_for_openpyxl_unavailable: Include CSV export of key computed columns and explicit cell references in guide; add screenshots of important workbook panes if possible.
verification_checklist_for_reviewer: Open the provided CSV snapshot and confirm 'needs_review' flagged records match the guide rules.
Open the XLSX and inspect the value_per_unit_cost formula uses evidence_count as cost proxy.
Confirm budget_share_usd recomputes to 250000.0 and that economic_rank ordering matches the evidence_count-based value_per_unit_cost.
Confirm final_score arithmetic is published and reproducible from rubric_item_scores and declared weights.
Confirm sensitivity recomputation and document any flips.

priority_items: {'rubric_item': 'ri_4', 'issue': 'Value-per-cost computed without using evidence_count as the explicit cost proxy; budget allocation uses severity proportions instead of evidence_count-based cost.'}

Open the XLSX and inspect the value_per_unit_cost formula uses evidence_count as cost proxy.
Confirm budget_share_usd recomputes to 250000.0 and that economic_rank ordering matches the evidence_count-based value_per_unit_cost.

planned_changes.description: Add 'needs_review' boolean column and 'needs_review_reason' text column to rubric_disagreement_matrix.xlsx. Implement formula: needs_review = OR(evidence_shortfall>0, contradiction_flag=TRUE) and populate reason as CONCAT of failed gates. Update the per-record recommendation logic to force 'needs_review' precedence: if needs_review TRUE then recommendation='needs_review' and action note is captured.

Replace value_per_cost formula with evidence_count-based cost proxy. New formulas to include in workbook and guide: expected_value = criterion_weight * evidence_count; per_record_cost = evidence_count (document units); value_per_unit_cost = expected_value / per_record_cost. Recompute economic_rank by sorting descending on value_per_unit_cost. Recompute budget_share_usd proportional to value_per_unit_cost or explicitly document chosen allocation formula based on evidence_count-based cost.

Document rubric scoring aggregation: add a section in adjudication_guide.md specifying per-rubric-item weights and show the arithmetic used to produce final_score. Recompute final_score to be reproducible from rubric_item_scores or adjust scores/weights so they match.

Add explicit cell references and named ranges in adjudication_guide.md (sheet names, column letters/ranges) for thresholds, gate formulas, 'Blocker' flag, 'needs_review' column, and ranking columns. Also include a CSV export of the computed result columns (record, gate_class, needs_review, severity_score, expected_value, value_per_unit_cost, economic_rank, budget_share_usd) to support verification without openpyxl.

Add tie-break rules to the guide for boundary conditions (e.g., evidence_count == limit_evidence_count) and implement them in workbook formulas. Re-run sensitivity analysis (+/- sensitivity_shift_pct) using updated cost proxy and include changed decision rows if any.

Run a local validation checklist: ensure release_count==expected, budget sum==decision_budget_usd, every record has single recommended label (or needs_review), and include a short verification log describing manual checks performed (screenshots or CSV).

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Remediate disqualifying omission (missing 'needs_review' flags), align economic ranking and budget allocation with rubric (use evidence_count as cost proxy), publish rubric aggregation weights, and produce verifier-friendly workbook documentation given openpyxl parsing limitations.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The submission includes the required artifacts and clear gate logic, but contains two actionable issues: (1) the adjudication materials do not mark records that are contradictory or under-supported as 'needs_review' (a disqualifying omission per the rubric); (2) the economic ranking/budget logic deviates from the rubric's requirement to use evidence_count as the cost proxy (value_per_cost uses severity-adjusted denominator and budget_share uses severity proportions). Additionally, the grader's reported numeric aggregation (final_score = 0.6) is not reproducible from listed rubric item scores; the workbook could not be parsed automatically (openpyxl unavailable), so verifiers must inspect the xlsx manually.
